Examples.Title: Token-based Authentication
Examples.Description: Generates a temporary JSON Web Token (JWT) with a 30-second (by default) TTL and usage::write permission for core voice APIs, requiring an API key with Member or higher authorization. Tokens created with this endpoint will not work with the Manage APIs.
Examples.Code:
### Token-based Authentication
# @name auth.v1.tokens.grant
POST {{host}}/v1/auth/grant
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json

{
  "ttl_seconds": 30
}

## Responses
# 200
# Description: Grant response
# Content-Type: application/json
# 400
# Description: Invalid Request
# Content-Type: application/json

Examples.Title: Create a Project Key
Examples.Description: Creates a new API key with specified settings for the project
Examples.Code:
### Create a Project Key
# @name manage.projects.keys.create
POST {{host}}/v1/projects/{{project_id}}/keys
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json

"string"

## Responses
# 200
# Description: API key created successfully
# Content-Type: application/json
# 400
# Description: Invalid Request
# Content-Type: application/json

Examples.Title: Get Project Usage
Examples.Description: Retrieves the usage for a specific project. Use Get Project Usage Breakdown for a more comprehensive usage summary.
Examples.Code:
### [DEPRECATED] Get Project Usage
# @name manage.v1.projects.usage.get
GET {{host}}/v1/projects/{{project_id}}/usage?start={{start}}&end={{end}}&accessor=12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012&alternatives=true&callback_method=true&callback=true&channels=true&custom_intent_mode=true&custom_intent=true&custom_topic_mode=true&custom_topic=true&deployment=hosted&detect_entities=true&detect_language=true&diarize=true&dictation=true&encoding=true&endpoint=listen&extra=true&filler_words=true&intents=true&keyterm=true&keywords=true&language=true&measurements=true&method=async&model=6f548761-c9c0-429a-9315-11a1d28499c8&multichannel=true&numerals=true&paragraphs=true&profanity_filter=true&punctuate=true&redact=true&replace=true&sample_rate={{sample_rate}}&search=true&sentiment=true&smart_format=true&summarize=true&tag=tag1&topics=true&utt_split=true&utterances=true&version=true
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
Accept: application/json

## Responses
# 200
# Description: A specific request for a specific project
# Content-Type: application/json
# 400
# Description: Invalid Request
# Content-Type: application/json

Examples.Title: Get Project Usage Breakdown
Examples.Description: Retrieves the usage breakdown for a specific project, with various filter options by API feature or by groupings. Setting a feature (e.g. diarize) to true includes requests that used that feature, while false excludes requests that used it. Multiple true filters are combined with OR logic, while false filters use AND logic.
Examples.Code:
### Get Project Usage Breakdown
# @name manage.v1.projects.usage.breakdown.get
GET {{host}}/v1/projects/{{project_id}}/usage/breakdown?start={{start}}&end={{end}}&grouping=accessor&accessor=12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012&alternatives=true&callback_method=true&callback=true&channels=true&custom_intent_mode=true&custom_intent=true&custom_topic_mode=true&custom_topic=true&deployment=hosted&detect_entities=true&detect_language=true&diarize=true&dictation=true&encoding=true&endpoint=listen&extra=true&filler_words=true&intents=true&keyterm=true&keywords=true&language=true&measurements=true&method=async&model=6f548761-c9c0-429a-9315-11a1d28499c8&multichannel=true&numerals=true&paragraphs=true&profanity_filter=true&punctuate=true&redact=true&replace=true&sample_rate={{sample_rate}}&search=true&sentiment=true&smart_format=true&summarize=true&tag=tag1&topics=true&utt_split=true&utterances=true&version=true
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
Accept: application/json

## Responses
# 200
# Description: Usage breakdown response
# Content-Type: application/json
# 400
# Description: Invalid Request
# Content-Type: application/json

Examples.Title: Get Project Billing Breakdown
Examples.Description: Retrieves the billing summary for a specific project, with various filter options or by grouping options.
Examples.Code:
using var client = new DeepgramClient(apiKey);
var response = await client.Agent.ListAsync();
Examples.Setup: This example assumes `using Deepgram;` is in scope and `apiKey` contains the required credential.

Examples.Title: List Project Requests
Examples.Description: Generates a list of requests for a specific project
Examples.Code:
using var client = new DeepgramClient(apiKey);
var response = await client.Agent.ListAsync();
Examples.Setup: This example assumes `using Deepgram;` is in scope and `apiKey` contains the required credential.

Examples.Title: Update a Project
Examples.Description: Updates the name or other properties of an existing project
Examples.Code:
### Update a Project
# @name manage.projects.update
PATCH {{host}}/v1/projects/{{project_id}}
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json

{
  "name": "string"
}

## Responses
# 200
# Description: A project
# Content-Type: application/json
# 400
# Description: Invalid Request
# Content-Type: application/json

Examples.Title: Text to Speech transformation
Examples.Description: Convert text into natural-sounding speech using Deepgram's TTS REST API
Examples.Code:
### Text to Speech transformation
# @name speak.v1.audio.generate
POST {{host}}/v1/speak?callback={{callback}}&callback_method=POST&mip_opt_out=false&tag={{tag}}&bit_rate=48000&container=wav&encoding=mp3&model=aura-2-thalia-en&sample_rate=24000
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json, application/octet-stream

{
  "text": "Hello, welcome to Deepgram!"
}

## Responses
# 200
# Description: Successful text-to-speech transformation
# Content-Type: application/octet-stream
# 400
# Description: Invalid Request
# Content-Type: application/json

## Callbacks
### [CALLBACK] Callback endpoint for text-to-speech results
# Name: speakCallback
# Target: {$request.query.callback}
# Method: POST
# Content-Type: application/octet-stream
## Responses
# 200
# Description: We expect a 200 to determine whether the callback was successfully received
